title: feat: added log_path support for configuration files
body:
Added support for log_path option in containers.conf, this allows users to set a default logging directory from the container configuration file. A log path is read from `[containers]` section in the .conf file. For each container a sub-directory is created using the container id as the name and a ctr.log file is created within that sub-directory. This option can be overridden if set, `--log-opt` flag will take priority.

This pr also includes some vendor updates.

a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

## What type of PR is this?
/kind feature

## Dependencies:
**This PR depends on containers/common#2491**


#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Add log_path configuration option to containers.conf allowing users to specify custom directories for container log files. Container logs are organized in subdirectories named by container ID with ctr.log files.
```
